opportunity.title: Show HN: ScrapAI – We scrape 500 sites. AI runs once per site, not per page
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The "once per site" architecture is an interesting cost optimization choice. I've been considering whether running AI inference once per domain and then applying those patterns to child pages cuts compute costs significantly, though it risks missing page-level nuances. What's your take on whether this scales well when sites have deeply nested content with different classification needs?
actions.reasoning: This directly addresses architectural decisions in AI tooling for web scraping, specifically the trade-off between site-level vs. per-page AI inference.
